# pfsense_firewall_nat_outbound_mapping (Resource)



## Example Usage

```terraform
# Outbound SNAT: rewrite the source address of traffic leaving WAN from the LAN
# subnet to the WAN interface address. Requires outbound NAT mode to be set to
# hybrid or manual on the firewall. Omitting `protocol` matches any protocol.
resource "pfsense_firewall_nat_outbound_mapping" "lan_to_wan" {
  interface   = "wan"
  source      = "10.0.10.0/24"
  destination = "any"
  target      = "wanip"
  descr       = "SNAT LAN clients behind the WAN address"

  # Stage the change without reloading the ruleset. A later resource that does
  # apply immediately (or a manual apply) commits the whole batch at once.
  apply_immediately = false
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `destination` (String) The destination network this rule should match. Valid value options are: an existing interface, a subnet CIDR, an existing alias, `any`, `pppoe`. The context of this address can be inverted by prefixing the value with `!`. For interface values, the `:ip`  modifier can be appended to the value to use the interface's IP address instead of its entire subnet.<br>
- `interface` (String) The interface on which traffic is matched as it exits the firewall. In most cases this is a WAN-type or another externally-connected interface.<br>
- `source` (String) The source network this rule should match. Valid value options are: an existing interface, a subnet CIDR, an existing alias, `any`, `(self)`, `pppoe`. The context of this address can be inverted by prefixing the value with `!`. For interface values, the `:ip`  modifier can be appended to the value to use the interface's IP address instead of its entire subnet.<br>
- `target` (String) The target network traffic matching this rule should be translated to. Valid value options are: an IP address, an existing alias. For interface values, the `:ip`  modifier can be appended to the value to use the interface's IP address instead of its entire subnet.<br><br>This field is only available when the following conditions are met:<br>- `nonat` must be equal to `false`<br>

### Optional

- `apply_immediately` (Boolean) Whether to apply the staged change to the running firewall immediately after this resource is created, updated, or deleted. Defaults to true. Set to false to batch multiple changes and apply them separately.
- `descr` (String) A description for the outbound NAT mapping.<br>
- `destination_port` (String) The destination port this rule should match. Valid options are: a TCP/UDP port number, a TCP/UDP port range separated by `:`, an existing port type firewall alias<br>
- `disabled` (Boolean) Disable this outbound NAT rule.<br>
- `nat_port` (String) The external source port or port range used for rewriting the original source port on connections matching the rule. Valid options are: a TCP/UDP port number, a TCP/UDP port range separated by `:`<br><br>This field is only available when the following conditions are met:<br>- `static_nat_port` must be equal to `false`<br>- `nonat` must be equal to `false`<br>
- `nonat` (Boolean) Do not NAT traffic matching this rule.<br>
- `nosync` (Boolean) Do not sync this rule to HA peers.<br>
- `poolopts` (String) The pool option used to load balance external IP mapping when `target` is set to a subnet or alias of many addresses. Set to `null` to revert to the system default.<br><br>This field is only available when the following conditions are met:<br>- `nonat` must be equal to `false`<br>
- `protocol` (String) The protocol this rule should match. Use `null` for any protocol.<br>
- `source_hash_key` (String) The key that is fed to the hashing algorithm in hex format. This must be a 16 byte (32 character) hex string prefixed with `0x`. If a value is not provided, one will automatically be generated<br><br>This field is only available when the following conditions are met:<br>- `poolopts` must be equal to `'source-hash'`<br>- `nonat` must be equal to `false`<br>
- `source_port` (String) The source port this rule should match. Valid options are: a TCP/UDP port number, a TCP/UDP port range separated by `:`, an existing port type firewall alias<br>
- `static_nat_port` (Boolean) Do not rewrite source port for traffic matching this rule.<br><br>This field is only available when the following conditions are met:<br>- `nonat` must be equal to `false`<br>
- `target_subnet` (Number) The subnet bits for the assigned `target`. This field is only applicable if `target` is set to an IP address. This has no affect for alias or interface `targets`.<br><br>This field is only available when the following conditions are met:<br>- `nonat` must be equal to `false`<br>

### Read-Only

- `id` (Number) The ID of the object to target.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Outbound NAT mappings are addressed by their position (0-based index) in
# pfSense's outbound mapping list — the same order shown under
# Firewall > NAT > Outbound.
terraform import pfsense_firewall_nat_outbound_mapping.lan_to_wan 0
```
